claims.statement: Patch benchmark schema v11 and matrix schema v5 add pass-through batch count, batched source-cell count and bytes, peak cells per batch and their existing parser/action/output telemetry context.

On this machine and the recorded one-million-cell level-1 targeted-upsert workloads, pass-through batching reduced transform actions and output append calls by 99.82%; numeric and formula-plus-hyperlink total editor medians decreased by 21.21% and 15.69%, while mixed-inline changed from 1205 ms to 1222 ms and is not claimed as a throughput gain.

The recorded profiles batched at least 999482 untouched source cells into 84-102 callback-lifetime spans with a peak of 25640-31974 cells per span; median process peak working set decreased by 0.21-0.22 MB and output pending buffers stayed below 29 KiB.

Final production and stored CTest passed 132/132; the no-images runtime smoke and production/stored/no-images install consumers passed. All representative outputs opened with openpyxl 3.1.2, and the formula profile preserved https://example.com/fastxlsx-benchmark. Office was not run, and no cross-machine, arbitrary-workbook, full-feature or OpenXLSX-superiority claim is made.
